original_hypothesis.title: TieredLiquidityDistributor prize size computation may produce zero/incorrect prize sizes for unsupported tier/numTiers combinations, breaking claims and causing operational dead zones
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Tier odds and canary prize count fractionals are hardcoded only for numTiers in [3..15]. If any pathway (misconfiguration or future extension) sets numberOfTiers outside these bounds or calls getTierOdds/estimatedPrizeCount with out-of-range values, odds return sd(0) and estimated prize count returns 0, which can drive winner calculations toward always-false and break fee computations/claim incentives. Although bounds are enforced in constructor/_nextDraw, operational tooling and external callers may rely on view outputs for off-chain automation and could mis-handle zeros, causing failed closeDraw/claimer pricing decisions.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is primarily an ops/automation correctness risk: many off-chain systems will call view functions like estimatedPrizeCount, getTierOdds, canaryPrizeCount, etc. If they ever pass an out-of-range numTiers (including during upgrades or testing), they may compute nonsensical outputs and submit bad transactions that revert or behave unexpectedly.
deployment_assumptions: Off-chain automation or external integrators call TieredLiquidityDistributor view functions with arbitrary numTiers values and use outputs for critical decisions without validating bounds
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: TieredLiquidityDistributor._tierOdds and _estimatedPrizeCount return default 0 for unsupported numTiers; only internal state is bounded, but external calls accept any numTiers.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Default fallthrough returns 0 in _estimatedPrizeCount/_canaryPrizeCountFractional/_tierOdds when numTiers unsupported

preconditions: External automation uses these functions with out-of-range numTiers, or a future code change bypasses bounds
Automation decisions depend on non-zero odds/prize counts

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Hardcoded lookup tables return 0 on unsupported inputs without explicit revert, making failures silent for callers.
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: During integration, monitoring, or if bounds enforcement is ever bypassed in future versions.

operational_questions: Do any bots/services call estimatedPrizeCount(numTiers) or getTierOdds(tier,numTiers) with non-state numTiers values?
Are callers validating that outputs are non-zero and inputs are within [3..15]?
Could a zero output propagate into a state-changing tx (e.g., VRGDA pricing inputs, closeDraw heuristics)?
validation_requirements: Review off-chain systems and SDKs for these calls and input validation
Add tests ensuring automation fails safely when receiving 0 odds/prize counts
Confirm no internal state path can reach out-of-range numTiers

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Identify off-chain automation relying on these views without bounds checks
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Trigger automation into using out-of-range inputs (e.g., by feeding it misleading state assumptions)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Cause automation to submit suboptimal or reverting transactions (DoS/operational disruption)

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Automation failures; incorrect fee pricing; stalled claiming/closing due to bad off-chain decisions
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: Claimers, monitoring, analytics, any bot-driven maintenance

execution_requirements_analysis.operational_timing: Most likely during integration changes or incident recovery

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Bots submitting reverting txs correlated with out-of-range parameters
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Sudden drop to zero in off-chain computed prize counts/odds
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Automation using hardcoded tier counts diverging from on-chain numberOfTiers

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
